story.text: # ⚖️ The Grammar Workshop — Lexon x the Privacy Value Model

What if the ideas behind a privacy architecture could be written in a language that is at once plain English, an executable contract, and a machine-checkable claim? This workshop expressed the **Privacy Value Model** (the theory behind 0xagentprivacy: two separated agents, a Swordsman who protects and a Mage who delegates, held apart by a Gap) term by term in **[Lexon](https://www.lexon.org)**, Henning Diedrich's controlled-English language for computational law.

The result of six days: **189 of the model's 211 canon terms are now expressed in a form whose load-bearing claim can be mechanically verified and falsified.** The census is 90 percent covered. The harness even wrote its own constitution in the language its own loop gates.

Source of truth: `github.com/mitchuski/lexon_pvm` (git). This wiki is a forkable projection that carries lineage.

## The reading path

Read these in order and the whole thing lands in about fifteen minutes; each stands alone if you prefer to jump.

1. 🔗 [[The Convergence — Lexon Meets The Privacy Value Model]] — what Lexon is, what the model is, and why a contract is already a knowledge graph.
2. ⿻ [[The Harness — The Model Turned On Itself]] — the dual-agent research loop that did the work, and why a third agent (the Gap) is the whole point.
3. 🛡️ [[The Gate — How A Claim Gets Proven]] — the grammar checker and the mutation probe that make "privacy is architectural" a mechanical fact.
4. 📉 [[The Results — Five Runs, 211 To 22]] — what crossed into constrained language, the two honest kills, the residue.
5. 📖 [[Reading The Language]] — how to read a term, a claim, a block, and a spell.

## Go deeper

- ⚖️ [[The Grammar Workshop]] — the repository overview and how to verify it yourself.
- 🪞 [[What This Means For Agentprivacy]] — the honest assessment of what the work moves forward.
- 🏛️ [[Capstone — The Grammar Workshop]] — the whole arc in one record.
- 🗺️ [[The Corpus Coverage Plan]] — the path from a covered index to a covered canon.
- 🧱 [[Structured Language Blocks]] — the deterministic write-path for a shared public knowledge graph.

## One paragraph, if you read nothing else

The Privacy Value Model is a theory of privacy as architecture: two software agents kept structurally separate so neither can learn what the other knows. This workshop expressed that model's canon in Lexon, a language that reads as plain English but parses to knowledge-graph triples, using a research loop built on the same two-agent design. Every expression carries a machine-checkable claim, so the model's promise that a boundary "cannot be crossed by design" becomes a query that provably returns nothing. What remains uncovered is the mathematical frontier and the deep story, left home on purpose.
